title: Recovering Podman Usability After Full Disk
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

At first there was a problem using podman after the root disk was filled by a running container (`/var/lib` not a separate mount).

```
[root@Centos7 ~]# podman ps -a
Error: error creating libpod runtime: mkdir /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/compat258837963: no space left on device
```

So I decided to clean up with the following commands assuming that every tool should recover from its contents in `/var` being not there:

```
[root@Centos7 ~]# sudo rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/*
[root@Centos7 ~]# sudo rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/*
```

However, it didn't fully recover. When I try to create a pod now, e.g. by building my [simpleserver](https://github.com/rhdojun/simpleserver) project, I get the following result:

```
Error: error creating build container: error creating container: error creating read-write layer with ID "d244d8fbcdd773c28ac7dc29a08c09358ea3c50db92acb1a6ef44d00eb0a0386": no such file or directory
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `[root@Centos7 code]# git clone https://github.com/rhdojun/simpleserver.git`

2. `[root@Centos7 code]# cd simpleserver/`

3. make:
```
[root@Centos7 simpleserver]# make
podman build -t simpleserver .
STEP 1: FROM centos:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 294dced27509 done  
Copying config c460095972 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: error creating build container: error creating container: error creating read-write layer with ID "d244d8fbcdd773c28ac7dc29a08c09358ea3c50db92acb1a6ef44d00eb0a0386": no such file or directory
make: *** [all] Error 125
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Podman errors out with: `Error: error creating build container: error creating container: error creating read-write layer with ID "d244d8fbcdd773c28ac7dc29a08c09358ea3c50db92acb1a6ef44d00eb0a0386": no such file or directory`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman creates the container and starts building the simpleserver project.
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.12
  podman version: 1.6.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.12.0-dev
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.8-1.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.8, commit: f85c8b1ce77b73bcd48b2d802396321217008762'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 32092590080
  MemTotal: 33524305920
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-67.rc10.el7_8.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 1073737728
  SwapTotal: 1073737728
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: <deleted>
  kernel: 3.10.0-1127.10.1.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 6h 56m 14.53s (Approximately 0.25 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.centos.org
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 15
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.4-18.el7_8.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

bare metal. Centos provided by server maintainer.

comments.body: I think that removing all of `/var/lib/containers/storage/` is probably necessary - otherwise, there are databases left behind that expect that the files you just removed still exist, and Podman will be very confused because of this.

That's it.

So the problem was not as I feared that I deleted too much. The problem was I didn't delete enough. All my pods are coming up again now. Thanks @mheon!
